title: Cotula mariae
uses: Cotula mariae is a flowering plant that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in sesquiterpenes, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, Cotula mariae has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin irritations, and digestive issues. Its roots and leaves are sometimes used in teas and infusions to aid in relieving colds and respiratory problems. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Cotula mariae as a medicinal treatment.
taxonomy: Cotula mariae is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its characteristics and shared traits with other plants in the same family. Cotula mariae is further classified within the subfamily Asteroideae and the tribe Inuleae. The plant's botanical classification also includes the genus Cotula, which comprises several species of annual or perennial herbs. Overall, the classification of Cotula mariae reflects its relationships and similarities with other plants in the Asteraceae family.
